content:
First of all, thanks for having me here!

You can call me Jynn. I'm currently still studying in college (in fact, I'm writing this during class), so I may not have all the time in the world... 😌 

As for my help, other than being confident in my English reading and writing skills... I also have experience working with Unity for my college course (it's game dev), so testing builds out won't be too difficult for me, I think?

Anyways, I love the game to bits as much as I criticize it and being part of this is as daunting as it is exciting ✨

Greetings to thee!
I be Adelle, come forth to lend mine aid as translator (EN to ID and vice versa), proofreader, and tester.
Lo, I report for duty, merrily so ~

Hello, I'm Mercy! I speak only English and live in the Pacific Timezone of the US *(UTC/GMT -7)*.

I started playing Morimens as recently as this month but I absolutely love this game and hope to see it grow. Being new however means I may not have in-game knowledge or experience to pull from so I may need to ask lots of questions *(which I absolutely will do)* to make sure I get everything perfectly correct.

I'm a long time TCG gamer and Magic: the Gathering Judge/Rules Advisor with a specialty in the templating, phrasing, and grammar of games. I'm a huge etymology and semantics nerd and I wanted to join the team because I saw many of those easily overlooked errors in the game and wished I could help, and here I am!

My primary focus is to help template the descriptions of Cards, Relics, Abilities, Effects, etc. consistently so that new players can easily understand what everything does; however, *(edit)*, now about a month in to this project, I've decided to take on a project coordination role, and I hope that I can help you all do your work smoother!
Thanks for having me! <:LUV:1003188300036702229>
-# and I may as well put this here cus why not: UID 101116367 Invite 0OZKS64MPU

Hi hi everyone, I’m Sophie and while I speak both English and Portuguese fluently, I’d feel more comfortable starting out with proofreading and localizing the former rather than the latter. (Also, I believe that tidying up the English translation is more likely to have a positive impact on the game’s reception and potential reach.)

Like Mercy, I’m a new player, having started roughly a month ago, and since then I’ve been completely hooked on both Morimens’ gameplay and world building. I noticed right away the many issues - both minor and severe - affecting the current translation; so I was delighted when BIAV opened up the volunteer applications.

My intention, once I’ve familiarized myself with the resources we have available, is to replay whichever chapter I’ll be focusing on, and making adjustments based on the context of each scene. Fixing any of those pesky mixed pronouns, making names consistent throughout, and trying to make everything sound more natural.

I’m delighted to be here and excited to start working on this project with all of you!

Hello!

I'm Lens on Discord, though I have gone by various different names depending on the community. I started Morimens quite recently (after Doresain banner started) and was absolutely blown away by the quality of the game. I was quite surprised to see how small the playerbase of this game was, and a bit of digging revealed that most people seem to be turned off by the lack of a proper human English translation. Given that, I jumped at the chance to assist with polishing up the English to an acceptable standard so that I can properly recommend Morimens to more people to hopefully spread the word, as I find it a cardinal sin for such an incredible game to go overlooked by so many people.

For my background, I have a rather extensive history of dabbling in Japanese to English fan translations across various media, as I tend to join translation projects whenever I see something I particularly like and want it to be spread to a larger audience. I originally began assisting with translation work around two decades ago, when I started helping with editing in fan-translated visual novel projects. As I learned Japanese, I later branched out to working as a translator with anime fansubbing groups and manga scanlation groups. In the past few years, I have primarily worked on mobile games, light novels, and webnovels. 

My experience exclusively lies in fan translation, not professional, as I lack the long-term motivation and drive to complete larger projects. I have worked primarily as a Japanese translator and English editor, with a strong focus on aggressive editing to ensure that the end result maintains stylistic consistency and matches the tone and voice of the original work. My primary goal is to ensure the main story of the game provides at minimum a coherent and consistent reading experience from start to finish, with a side goal of ensuring that translated text measures up to the rather lyrical and prosaic writing style the game is going for.

Ayo!!!! 
My name's Matt - I will be helping the community as a proofreader and tester
I had some past experiences

Thanks :3 cya around
Hi, I'm Dredge. I'm currently working on an EN "wiki" for the game, so I'm fairly familiar with what we'll be doing for the game. Looking forward to working with you all.
Good morning, my name is Marcos, already have some experience with proofreading and tester, nice to meet you all and I hope we get along well.

Hello everyone, I'm Noah!

I'm working from home as an artist. I'm lucky enough to currently have flexible hours and a lot of time to dedicate to Morimens.

I speak English and French, and have some notions of Japanese which I'm still currently studying. I am also planning to learn Chinese. I have some experience in fan translation, specifically of manga chapters that weren't licensed at the time, from both Japanese to English/French, and English to French.

I can work as a translator, currently mainly from English to French, although my goal is to eventually be able to help from Chinese to French directly. I can also help on the proofreading and testing side, for both English and French.

I love this game, and I'm looking forward to working with you all!

Hi, I'm Julia <:hello:1356053084790460437> 

I work for a German publishing house, and one of my responsibilities is translating articles GER > ENG for the internal knowledge database. I also am currently studying Digital Media and Technology.

I speak Polish, English and German and will be helping with proofreading and testing for mainly the German version of the game.

I hope our efforts can help the game flourish more and reach the audience it deserves; I'm very thrilled to be working with you all!

Hello everyone, pleased to see so many of you here with me 🥺 
I'm Goz, a French guy working from home as a software engineer, relatively new player of Morimens (~4months).
I've read shitty MTL for a ton of mangas so I've developed a resistance to it and enjoyed the story, but since there is an occasion to help I'm here for it.

I'll be mainly serving as a proofreader & tester for French & English, & as a moderator in here to try and help people organize.

If you have troubles with git or github, feel free to reach out, as I know a fair bit about both.

Hello! I'm Shirogane!
I am a Japanese speaker, but my second languages are English and Chinese.
For this reason, I usually play Morimens in simplified Chinese.
I got involved in this project because a friend recommended this game to me. She usually plays the Japanese version translated by ChatGPT, and was often troubled by inconsistencies in the translation of names and the storyline.
I myself once switched to the Japanese version, but gave up after encountering several parts where the sentences didn't make sense.
I want to deliver the detailed and beautiful storyline and experience of the original to passionate Japanese users like her. 
Also, since many Japanese people are reluctant to play games that don't have Japanese translations, I'll do my best to get them interested! (Even though the Japanese version has been released, we have confirmed that some people prefer to play the Steam version due to the different character abilities and gameplay experience!)

By the way, Personally, I really love the Cthulhu Mythos motif and DnD, so I really want this game to become more popular!
